free_text: Activation cross-sections of deuteron induced
reactions on natural palladium
High purity, natural 0.0125 mm Pd foils (Goodfellow)
were used interleaved with Al 0.1 mm monitor foils.
The irradiations
took place at a constant beam current of 200 nA
for 30 min at VUB and 110 nA for 34 min at CYRIC
respectively. The incident beam energies were 15.1,
20.5 and 40 MeV.
The radioactivity of each sample and
monitor foil was measured nondestructively by HPGe
spectrometry twice. A first counting was started
in Brussels shortly after EOB while in Sendai a
cooling period of at least a day was applied
due to the high induced activity.
The decay data were taken from NUDAT.
Chapter 4
The uncertainty of the energy of the
bombarding beam in the middle of each irradiated foil
was estimated combining the uncertainties of the
energy degradation depending on the thickness of the
target foils in the stack and the energy straggling.
The uncertainty on the median energy in the last foil
was +-0.8 MeV for the irradiations at VUB while it
amounted to +-1.5 MeV for the last foil of the long
tacks at CYRIC.
The uncertainties of the cross-section
values were estimated in the standard way: the
independent relative errors of the linearly
contributing processes were summed quadratically and
the square root of the sum was taken. The average
uncertainty was found  to be 10-14%.
number of target nuclei (5%);
detector efficiency (10%);
beam current(7%);
statistical and peak fitting (5%)
The experimental data were compared to the
results of the theoretical model codes TALYS, EPIRE-D
and ALICE-D.
The measured experimental data were compared to
those found in the literature.
Fitted excitation functions were used for
determination of integral physical yields.
